title: Readline Promises, Stream Injection, EOF, Abort, and Close Ownership · Compiler Case: Pokedex Ts Question Abort
summary: The loop creates multiple interfaces, reads global process streams inside domain code, ignores EOF and rejection, leaves question promises pending, and closes streams it does not own. Predict the user decision, runtime value, strict type claim, promise state, API boundary, failure, and observable evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the pokedex ts question abort increment in injected abortable readline session adapter. Learners progress through predicti…
objectives: Pass an AbortSignal to pending questions, classify abort separately, and remove or one-shot listeners without leaks.
Create one readline promises interface from injected input and output and distinguish interface ownership from stream ownership.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Predict Pokedex Ts Question Abort
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory TypeScript team handles case 9832 during an inclusive museum learning kiosk. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to prove one readline interface, abortable questions, EOF handling, ownership, and idempotent close; browser TypeScript must remain deterministic and free of Node or network effects; then inject one redirect, prototype-like key, terminal control, path, file, or signal failure and reject it safely. Browser code uses pure bounded runtime models and original fixtures only. Real PokéAPI traffic, Node readline, host streams, signals, files, package installs, handle and load claims, accessibility review, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Pass an AbortSignal to pending questions, classify abort separately, and remove or one-shot listeners without leaks.

steps.title: Build Pokedex Ts Question Abort evidence
steps.instruction: Append a compile-ready pure-TypeScript function headed "// Evidence: pokedex-ts-question-abort-v80ov9" that uses case 9031 to prove one readline interface, abortable questions, EOF handling, ownership, and idempotent close. Browser code must not import Node APIs, open streams or sockets, contact PokéAPI, read or write host files, handle real signals, leave timers or Promise rejections, or claim native runtime, load, accessibility, packaging, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with TypeScript 7.0.2, the TypeScript 6.0.2 compatibility surface where required, Node 24.18.0, original unknown fixtures, controlled fetch and stream adapters, emitted-runtime and subprocess tests, temporary files, package inspection, representative platforms, accessibility review, capacity, rollback, recovery, and release gates.
